tasks: List all subjects with low marks and identify recurring weak topics
Request previous year question papers from faculty/seniors
Create a subject-wise study timetable with daily 3-hour blocks
Join a subject-specific study group or find a peer tutor
weeks.goal: Have a clear map of what to study and a working timetable

tasks: Re-study Chapter 1 fundamentals of your weakest 2 subjects
Watch NPTEL/YouTube lectures at 1.5x speed for concept clarity
Solve 10 basic problems per subject daily
Take handwritten notes — proven to improve retention by 40%
weeks.goal: Clear conceptual doubts in at least 1 subject

tasks: Solve 5 years of previous exam papers for 2 subjects
Time yourself — simulate actual exam conditions
Review wrong answers immediately and note the concept gap
Prepare a 1-page formula/shortcut cheatsheet per subject
weeks.goal: Attempt at least 3 full mock papers

tasks: Visit professors during office hours — ask specific questions
Request internal test re-evaluation if marks were unfair
Complete all pending assignments and lab records
Submit extra assignments or project work to improve internal marks
weeks.goal: Secure maximum internal/continuous assessment marks

tasks: Submit a mini-project or add-on assignment to show initiative
Participate in department seminars and note attendance-linked marks
Review your current internal marks and calculate required end-sem score
Focus heavy revision on high-weightage chapters
weeks.goal: Know exact score needed from end-sem exam

tasks: Revise all chapters using active recall — no passive reading
Create topic-wise mind maps for each subject
Solve 20 MCQs/day from online test portals (TCS NQT, GeeksforGeeks)
Form a small study group for peer testing
weeks.goal: Complete at least 80% of syllabus revision

tasks: Take one full-length mock exam per day
Analyze and correct mistakes within 30 minutes of completing test
Focus on speed + accuracy — manage time per question
Revise weak chapters based on mock results
weeks.goal: Score 70%+ in mock exams consistently

tasks: No new topics — only revision of already-studied material
Sleep 7-8 hours — critical for memory consolidation
Read your cheatsheets and formula lists daily
Visualize scoring high and stay calm
weeks.goal: Perform at peak in actual examinations

tasks: Take a full TCS NQT diagnostic test online
Identify which sections you're weakest in: Quant, LR, or Verbal?
Bookmark IndiaBix.com and set daily goal of 30 questions
Download free aptitude app (Pocket Aptitude or PrepInsta)
weeks.goal: Know your current aptitude baseline score

tasks: Study: Number System, LCM/HCF, Percentage, Profit/Loss
Solve 20 quant problems daily on IndiaBix
Learn shortcut tricks for multiplication, division, and squares
Watch R.S. Aggarwal topic videos on YouTube
weeks.goal: Achieve 70% accuracy in Quant fundamentals

tasks: Study: Time & Work, Speed-Distance-Time, Mixtures, Ratios
Solve 25 problems/day across these topics
Take 1 timed quant mini-test (20 questions in 15 minutes)
Review all wrong answers on the same day
weeks.goal: Complete top 10 high-frequency aptitude chapters

tasks: Study: Blood Relations, Seating Arrangement, Syllogisms, Coding-Decoding
Solve 30 LR questions/day on PrepInsta or GeeksforGeeks
Take one LR-focused mock test
Practice with Puzzles from TCS NQT and Infosys sample papers
weeks.goal: Complete LR section with 75%+ accuracy

tasks: Study: Reading Comprehension, Fill-in-the-blanks, Sentence correction
Read one English news article daily (The Hindu or Economics Times)
Learn 10 new vocabulary words/day using Memrise or Anki
Practice 20 verbal questions/day on IndiaBix
weeks.goal: Achieve 70% accuracy in verbal/English sections

tasks: Take 1 full TCS NQT/Infosys/Wipro mock test daily
Analyze score, topic-wise accuracy, and time taken
Review category-wise performance — focus on weakest section
Aim to reduce per-question time to under 60 seconds
weeks.goal: Score 75+ consistently in full mock tests

no_internships.title: Internship Acquisition Plan
no_internships.target: Land your first internship within 4 weeks

tasks: Create/update LinkedIn profile with a professional photo and bio
Upload resume to Internshala, Naukri, and LinkedIn
Create a GitHub profile and upload any existing mini projects
Draft a strong internship cover letter template
weeks.goal: Have an active LinkedIn & Internshala profile live

tasks: Apply to 10 internships/day on Internshala (target remote/virtual roles)
Use filters: 0–1 year experience, remote, stipend ≥ ₹5000
Connect with 5 alumni from your college working in target companies on LinkedIn
Check company career pages of top 20 Indian companies for intern openings
weeks.goal: Apply to at least 50 internships by end of week

tasks: Complete one small project relevant to your target internship domain
Upload project on GitHub with a clear README
Write a LinkedIn post about something you built or learned
Prepare answers for common internship interview questions
weeks.goal: Have at least 1 project live on GitHub to showcase

tasks: Practice mock interviews using Pramp or InterviewBuddy
Research each company before attending their interview
Follow up on applications via email 48 hours after submitting
Consider applying to college-offered internship programs via TPO
weeks.goal: Secure at least 1 internship offer or project work

low_soft_skills.title: Communication & Soft Skills Plan
low_soft_skills.target: Build confident communication for interviews and workplace

tasks: Record yourself speaking for 2 minutes on any topic — review the recording
Take a free communication assessment on Coursera or Udemy
List your top 3 weaknesses: clarity, confidence, vocabulary, or speed
Start reading English news (The Hindu) for 15 minutes daily
weeks.goal: Clearly know which communication areas to improve

tasks: Join a Toastmasters club or online speaking group
Give a 3-minute speech on any topic daily — record and review
Learn 5 new English words/day — use in conversation
Practice STAR (Situation-Task-Action-Result) answers for HR questions
weeks.goal: Be able to speak fluently on any topic for 3 minutes

tasks: Practice 20 common HR questions (Tell me about yourself, strengths, weaknesses)
Participate in mock GD sessions (college, online groups, or YouTube GD topics)
Watch 5 successful interview videos on YouTube for body language tips
Learn the STAR method and practice 5 behavioral questions using it
weeks.goal: Confidently answer 20 common interview questions

tasks: Learn professional email writing — practice writing 3 emails/day
Improve LinkedIn profile with recommendations and endorsements
Practice confident body language: eye contact, posture, handshake
Do a final mock interview with a friend or career counselor
weeks.goal: Be interview-ready across verbal, written, and body language

backlogs.title: Backlog Clearing Strategy
backlogs.target: Clear all backlogs in next 60 days

tasks: Research: which companies filter by backlog count at placement drives?
Check your current semester regulations — what's the deadline to clear?
Talk to faculty in charge of supplementary exams
List all pending backlog subjects with their exam dates
weeks.goal: Have a clear plan for clearing all backlogs before placement season

tasks: Identify which backlog subjects are easiest to pass quickly
Download previous 5 years question papers for each subject
Focus only on high-probability exam questions — 80/20 rule
Watch YouTube lectures for fundamental concepts (1.5x speed)
weeks.goal: Study plan created for each backlog subject

tasks: Spend 4-5 hours/day on backlog subjects — group study if beneficial
Solve past papers under timed conditions
Create concise note cards for formulas, key definitions
Talk to seniors who've cleared the same backlogs for tips
weeks.goal: Cover 80% of syllabus for at least 1 backlog subject

tasks: Simulate exam conditions: sit for 3 hours with only your notes excluded
Grade yourself honestly using answer keys or faculty help
Identify patterns in your errors and revise those topics
Prepare exam strategy: which questions to attempt first
weeks.goal: Ready to appear in supplementary/backlog exams

low_projects.title: Project Portfolio Builder Plan
low_projects.target: Add 3 quality projects to your portfolio in 6 weeks

tasks: Choose 3 project ideas from the PlaceIQ Project Suggestions page
Create GitHub account and learn basic Git commands (push, pull, commit)
Watch a 'How to build a portfolio project' YouTube video
Set up your development environment (VS Code, Python, etc.)
weeks.goal: Have 3 project ideas chosen and GitHub account ready

tasks: Build your simplest project first — aim for a working prototype in 5 days
Push all code to GitHub daily — commit with descriptive messages
Add a README.md with screenshot, what the project does, and how to run it
Show it to 1 friend or mentor for feedback
weeks.goal: Project 1 live on GitHub with README

tasks: Build an intermediate project related to your stream/target role
Add a demo GIF or screenshot to README
Deploy project online (Heroku, Render, or Vercel — all free)
Write a short LinkedIn post about what you built
weeks.goal: Project 2 live on GitHub + deployed online

tasks: Build a project that solves a real problem — bonus if domain-specific
Add data visualizations, API integration, or ML model to increase complexity
Record a 2-minute demo video and upload to LinkedIn
Submit project to a relevant GitHub topic to increase visibility
weeks.goal: Project 3 live with demo video on LinkedIn

tasks: Update LinkedIn with all 3 projects in the 'Projects' section
Add projects to your resume under a 'Projects' section with 2-3 bullet points each
Get at least 2 GitHub stars by sharing projects in relevant forums or Reddit
Connect with recruiters on LinkedIn and mention your project work
weeks.goal: All 3 projects visible on LinkedIn and resume

tasks: Practice explaining each project in 2 minutes: What? Why? How? Results?
Prepare for common questions: 'What challenges did you face?', 'What would you improve?'
Review the code you wrote — be ready to explain every function
Add 1 more feature/improvement to your best project
weeks.goal: Confidently present all 3 projects in any interview
